name: ExtensionSubscription_Parameter
title: R5: Channel type (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Subscription.parameter` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Subscription.parameter` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Subscription.parameter` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Subscription.parameter` has a context of Subscription based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Subscription`.
Element `Subscription.parameter` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `Subscription.parameter` has a context of Subscription based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Subscription`.
Element `Subscription.parameter` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Exactly what these mean depend on the channel type. They can convey additional information to the server or recipient and/or meet security requirements; for example, support of multiple headers in the outgoing notifications for rest-hook type subscriptions. Note that names are not required to be unique, but channel definitions can impose restrictions.

Element `Subscription.parameter.name` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Subscription.parameter` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `Subscription.parameter.name` has a context of Subscription based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Subscription`.
Element `Subscription.parameter.name` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
element.requirements:
Element `Subscription.parameter.name` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Subscription.parameter` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `Subscription.parameter.name` has a context of Subscription based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Subscription`.
Element `Subscription.parameter.name` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: Parameter name for information passed to the channel for notifications, for example in the case of a REST hook wanting to pass through an authorization header, the name would be Authorization.

element.short: R5: Value of the parameter to use or pass through (new)
element.comment:

Element `Subscription.parameter.value` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Value of the parameter to use or pass through
element.definition: Parameter value for information passed to the channel for notifications, for example in the case of a REST hook wanting to pass through an authorization header, the value would be `Bearer 0193...`.
